groupShipActorId.name: Group Ship
groupShipActorId.hint: Select a starship or small craft actor to serve as the party's group ship. This setting is available for other features to reference.
groupShipActorId.hintOfficersLog: This setting is managed by the STA Officer's Log module. Configure it there.

enableTraitTokens.name: Enable Trait Tokens
enableTraitTokens.hint: Drag trait items onto the canvas to create trait drawings. Requires a world reload when changed.

worldTraitsActorUuid.name: World Traits Actor
worldTraitsActorUuid.hint: UUID of the Scene Traits actor to open from the World Traits tool button. Supports compendium actors. Leave blank to auto-create a world-level actor.

showInfoButtons.name: Show Info Buttons
showInfoButtons.hint: Show info buttons next to Stress Track, Determination, and Values headers that display quick reference popups.

enableFatigue.name: Enable Fatigue Management
enableFatigue.hint: Enable automatic fatigue trait creation when stress is maxed, fatigue notices in chat and dice pool dialogs, and fatigued attribute display on character sheets. Requires a world reload when changed.

enableExtendedTaskTracker.name: Enable Extended Task Tracker
enableExtendedTaskTracker.hint: Display bar-style extended task trackers on the canvas with breakthrough markers at 50% and 75%. GM can add, edit, and remove trackers. Requires a world reload when changed.

npcBuilderSpecialRulesPack.name: NPC Builder — Special Rules Compendium
npcBuilderSpecialRulesPack.hint: Pack ID of the compendium to use for special rules (e.g. "world.my-talents" or "module.my-module.talents"). When empty, the NPC Builder shows manual name/description fields instead.

enablePersonalThreat.name: Enable Personal Threat Dots
enablePersonalThreat.hint: Replace the resource bar on NPC tokens with a ring of red dots around the token edge — one dot per point of Bar 1 value. Requires a world reload when changed.

playerAmbientAudioSelectionOnly.name: Player Ambient Audio Uses Selected Tokens
playerAmbientAudioSelectionOnly.hint: If enabled, non-GM users only hear ambient audio near tokens they currently have selected/controlled (matching GM behavior). If disabled, Foundry's default behavior applies (players hear audio near all owned tokens).

enableBacklinks.name: Enable Journal Backlinks
enableBacklinks.hint: Add wiki-style 'referenced by' links to journals, actors, and items that reference each other. Requires a world reload when changed.

enableStyleEnhance.name: Enable Style Enhancements
enableStyleEnhance.hint: Apply additional CSS styling tweaks to STA sheets and chat cards. Takes effect immediately.

enableTalentAutomations.name: Enable Talent Automations
enableTalentAutomations.hint: Automatically apply effects based on talents a character possesses (e.g. modify attributes, post reminders). Requires a world reload when changed.

disableTooltips.name: Disable Item Tooltips
disableTooltips.hint: Hide the item description tooltip popups that appear when hovering over item names on actor sheets.

enableActionChooser.name: Enable Conflict Actions Window
enableActionChooser.hint: Show a window listing available actions, roll info, and Momentum costs during conflict scenes. Requires a world reload when changed.

actionChooserAsTab.name: Action Chooser as Sheet Tab
actionChooserAsTab.hint: Embed the action chooser as a tab on the character sheet instead of opening it as a separate window.

enableDicePoolOverride.name: Enable Dice Pool Override
enableDicePoolOverride.hint: Replace the system's dice pool dialog with an enhanced version that supports a middleware pipeline for talent automations, momentum spend, and other features. Requires a world reload when changed.

enableMomentumSpend.name: Enable Momentum Spend
enableMomentumSpend.hint: Add a 'Spend Momentum' button to task roll chat messages that opens a momentum spend dialog. Requires a world reload when changed.

autoDeductMomentum.name: Auto-deduct Momentum
autoDeductMomentum.hint: Automatically deduct momentum from the shared pool when spending via the Momentum Spend dialog.

enableMomentumMerger.name: Enable Momentum Merger
enableMomentumMerger.hint: Automatically merge consecutive momentum/threat tracker messages in chat into a single expandable message. Requires a world reload when changed.

enableChatHeaderMerge.name: Enable Chat Header Merge
enableChatHeaderMerge.hint: Hide the message header (avatar, name, timestamp) on consecutive chat messages from the same sender. Requires a world reload when changed.

sheetVariant.name: Character Sheet Style
sheetVariant.hint: Choose a visual style for character sheets. LCARS applies a futuristic Star Trek panel theme. Requires a world reload when changed.

lcarsColorScheme.name: LCARS Color Scheme
lcarsColorScheme.hint: Choose a color palette for the LCARS theme. Each scheme is inspired by a different Star Trek era. Only applies when LCARS Sheet is enabled.

mobileSheetTheme.name: Mobile Sheet Theme (Default)
mobileSheetTheme.hint: Default color palette for the mobile character sheet when no per-character theme has been chosen. Each character can override this individually via the palette icon in its sheet header. Only applies to Character (2e) Mobile sheets.

traits.fatiguedTooltip: Mark this Trait as a Fatigue trait (auto-created when stress is maxed).
traits.chooseAttributeTooltip: Choose which attribute your character is fatigued in.

stress.breatherDesc: Rest for a few minutes. Recover 4 stress.
stress.breakDesc: Rest for 30 minutes to a couple hours. Recover 8 Stress.
stress.sleepDesc: A full night's rest or similar amount of time. Recover all stress.
stress.momentumDesc: Recover 1 stress per 2 Momentum spent after a successful task, max 3.

determination.gainSpend: Gain or Spend Determination by using values.
determination.spendCrit: Spend determination before rolling for a guaranteed crit.
determination.spendReroll: Spend determination after rolling to reroll any failed dice.

valuesInfo.intro: Use Values to gain or spend determination:
valuesInfo.positiveDesc: Spend determination when your value is motivating you.
valuesInfo.negativeDesc: Gain determination when your value causes a complication.
valuesInfo.challengedDesc: Gain determination when you act against your values and change them.

talentsInfo.desc: Talents give you special abilities that let you do things outside the normal rules.

focusesInfo.desc: If a focus applies, dice will crit when they are equal to or less than your discipline used in the task.

traitsInfo.intro: If a trait is both TRUE and IMPORTANT, it can:

injuriesInfo.desc: Injuries are negative traits. Removing them requires a task roll with a difficulty equal to the severity of the attack that caused them.

logsInfo.desc2: Logs track which values you used in a mission.
logsInfo.desc3: String together multiple missions using the same value by making a callback during play to earn milestone advancements.

milestonesInfo.desc1: Milestones are improvements to your character.
milestonesInfo.desc2: Earn them by making a callback to another mission using the same value as the current task roll.

directiveInfo.desc: Directives are group values, and can be used in all the same ways as a value. Ask the GM what your Mission Directives are.

dicePoolMonitor.title: Dice Pool Monitor
dicePoolMonitor.gmOnly: Only the GM can use the dice pool monitor.
dicePoolMonitor.waitingForPlayers: Waiting for players to open dice pool dialogs…

warpCalculator.title: Warp Speed Calculator
warpCalculator.instructions: Enter any two values and leave the third blank to calculate it.
warpCalculator.enterTwoValues: Enter any two values above to calculate the third.
warpCalculator.cannotCalculate: Cannot calculate with the given values.

stardateCalculator.title: Stardate Calculator
stardateCalculator.instructions: Convert between TNG stardates and calendar dates.
stardateCalculator.enterDate: Enter a calendar date above to calculate the stardate.
stardateCalculator.enterStardate: Enter a stardate above to calculate the calendar date.

sceneTraitsActor.name: Scene Traits Actor
sceneTraitsActor.hint: Choose an existing Scene Traits actor for this scene, or leave as auto-create to generate one when first needed.

traitDrawingSettings.title: Trait Drawing Style
traitDrawingSettings.fontSizeHint: Percentage of grid size (e.g. 100 = one grid square tall).

rebuildOnSave.name: Rebuild links on save
rebuildOnSave.hint: Automatically rebuild reference links whenever a journal page, actor, or item is saved.

headingTag.name: Backlinks heading tag
headingTag.hint: HTML heading element for the backlinks section (e.g. h1, h2, h3).

minPermission.name: Backlinks minimum permission
minPermission.hint: A user needs at least this permission level on the target document for a backlink to be visible.

crewManifest.folderHint: The manifest will include all actors in this folder and its subfolders, grouped and color-coded by subfolder.
crewManifest.customizeHint: Check or uncheck categories and individual crew members to include in the manifest.
crewManifest.subtitle: Crew Manifest
crewManifest.noFolders: No Actor folders found. Create a folder of actors first.
crewManifest.noActors: No actors found in the selected folder.
crewManifest.folderNotFound: The selected folder could not be found.

createOpportunity.name: Create Opportunity
createOpportunity.description: Before you attempt a task (but after the GM sets its Difficulty), build your dice pool, starting with 2d20. Add bonus d20s granted from talents and other game effects. Add more d20s by spending Momentum or adding Threat. The first bonus die costs 1, the second costs 2 more, and the third costs 3 more. You can't roll more than 5d20 on any task attempt.

alterTrait.name: Create/Change Trait
alterTrait.description: You can establish a new trait in the scene, or change, increase the Potency of, or remove a trait currently in play. A trait you create must relate to the task you just passed, and it must be something that would result from your actions.

reduceTime.name: Reduce Time
reduceTime.description: You can spend 2 Momentum to reduce the time it takes to complete the activity represented by the task, typically taking half the normal amount of time. You can't use this option during an action scene.

extraMajorAction.name: Extra Major Action
extraMajorAction.description: During your turn in an action scene, you may spend 2 Momentum to take an additional major action. However, any task roll attempted as part of this action increases in Difficulty by 1.

extraMinorAction.name: Extra Minor Action
extraMinorAction.description: During your turn in an action scene, you may spend 1 Momentum to take an additional minor action. Each minor action may only be performed once per turn.

addedSeverity.name: Added Severity
addedSeverity.description: Increase the severity of a successful attack by 1 for every 2 Momentum spent.

buyD20s.name: Buy D20s
buyD20s.description: Before attempting a task (but after the GM sets its Difficulty), start your dice pool with 2d20. Add bonus d20s granted from talents and other game effects. Add more d20s by spending Momentum or adding Threat. The first bonus die costs 1, the second costs 2 more, and the third costs 3 more. You can't roll more than 5d20 on a task attempt.

disarm.name: Disarm
disarm.description: Target drops one weapon they are holding to within their Reach. This costs 1 Momentum if the weapon is held in one hand. If the weapon is two-handed, the cost increases to 2 Momentum.

keepInitiative.name: Keep The Initiative
keepInitiative.description: At the end of your turn, pass the next turn to an ally instead of an enemy. Once that ally has acted, the next turn must go to an enemy.

obtainInformation.name: Obtain Information
obtainInformation.description: You can ask the GM a single question about the scene. The question must be related to the task attempted, and the GM must answer truthfully but doesn't have to give complete information. If there's no information your character could determine, the GM may answer 'you don't know,' but they must refund the Momentum spent for that question.

recoveringStress.name: Recover Stress
recoveringStress.description: After a successful task, you may spend 2 Momentum to remove 1 Stress (Repeatable) from yourself or one ally who can hear you. A character cannot remove more than 3 Stress in this way on any single task.

addedDamage.name: Added Damage
addedDamage.description: Increase the damage of a successful attack by 1 for every 2 Momentum spent.

devastatingAttack.name: Devastating Attack
devastatingAttack.description: Roll an additional system. That system suffers a hit dealing half the attack's damage, rounding up.

actionChooser.title: Conflict Actions
actionChooser.noActor: Select a token or set an active character before using an action.
actionChooser.noStarship: Select a starship before using this action.
actionChooser.unlinkedTokenWarning: The selected starship has an unlinked prototype token. Changes made via unlinked tokens may not sync properly with the Action Chooser. Consider linking the token in the prototype token settings.

persuade.name: Persuade
persuade.description:
Opposed Task. Your roll sets the Difficulty and the target rolls to resist being persuaded.

Success, the target must either comply or resist by taking Stress equal to your successes. You may spend 2 Momentum (Repeatable) to increase the Stress by 1

Failure: If the target resists, they may not be persuaded again until the circumstance changes (usually by introducing a new trait or social tool).
persuade.chatSummary: Opposed task. Active character sets the Difficulty. Target must comply or take Stress equal to your successes.

evidence.name: Evidence
evidence.description: Offer proof to support your claims. Each piece of evidence is a trait, allowing a Persuasion task to happen or reducing its Difficulty by 1. Evidence can complement other social tools and each accepted piece adds 1 to the severity after an opposed Persuade task.
evidence.chatSummary: Enables a Persuade task or reduces its Difficulty by 1. Adds 1 to severity.

intimidation.name: Intimidation
intimidation.description:
Use threats to compel action. The active character rolls to set the difficulty, representing their percieved stregnth. The target rolls to resist being intimidated.

Success: Impose a fear-based trait on the target.
Failure: Further intimidation attempts come at +1 Difficulty.
intimidation.chatSummary: Opposed task. Success imposes a fear trait. Failure makes further intimidation harder.

negotiation.name: Negotiation
negotiation.description:
Offer a trade. No roll is required to negotiate. Each offer creates:
  • a trait representing what you give
  • a complication representing its cost to you
This could create new circumstances to facilitate a Persuade task, or even bypass the need for a roll entirely.

Note that the Negotiation traits don't define what the target character gives in return, other than being willing to speak to you or lowering the difficulty. Failing to honor your end of the deal will have consequences.
negotiation.chatSummary: Creates a trait (the offer) and a complication (the cost), counting as a new circumstance for Persuade.

deception.name: Deception
deception.description:
Convince someone of something untrue. You roll to set the Difficulty and the target rolls to see through the lie.

Success: Creates a trait representing the false belief. Each successful lie increases severity and complication range of Persuade tasks by 1 each.

Failure: If the target discovers the deception, the trait is lost and replaced by a complication.
deception.chatSummary: Opposed task. Each lie adds 1 to Persuade severity but also +1 complication range.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.
interact.chatSummary: Interact with an object. Complex tasks need a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.
prepare.chatSummary: Ready yourself for a rolled task.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.
restore.chatSummary: Resolve minor issues with ship systems.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help 2 other characters per round instead of just 1. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.
assist.chatSummary: Assist up to 2 allies per round.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description:
Roll vs Difficulty 2 to:
  • create a trait
  • change a trait
  • remove a trait
Examples include: battle plans, strategies, inspiration, etc.
createTrait.chatSummary: Difficulty 2. Create, change, or remove a trait.

override.name: Override
override.description: Take an action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.
override.chatSummary: Take a non-Command station action at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.
ready.chatSummary: Defer a major action until a chosen trigger occurs.

direct.name: Direct
direct.description: Spend 1 Momentum and select one ally on the bridge who immediately attempts a single Major Action. Assist them by rolling 1d20 with your Control + Command.
direct.chatSummary: Spend 1 Momentum. An ally takes a major action. Assist with Control + Command.

rally.name: Rally
rally.description: Roll Presence + Command vs Difficulty 0. Generate momentum to spend or save.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.
interact.chatSummary: Interact with an object. Complex tasks need a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.
prepare.chatSummary: Ready yourself for a rolled task.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.
restore.chatSummary: Resolve minor issues with ship systems.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description:
Roll vs Difficulty 2 to
  • create a trait
  • change a trait
  • remove a trait
Examples include: coordinating personnel, piercing interference, encryption and decryption.
createTrait.chatSummary: Difficulty 2. Create, change, or remove a trait.

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.
override.chatSummary: Take a non-Command station action at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.
ready.chatSummary: Defer a major action until a chosen trigger occurs.

damageControl.name: Damage Control
damageControl.description: Direct a team to patch a breach. Roll Presence + Engineering vs Difficulty 2, plus 1 for each degree of potency. Breach is temporarily removed on success.

transport.name: Transport
transport.description:
Beam people or objects over distances. Roll Control + Engineering assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science. Base Difficulty 0.
  • +1 if away from transporter room.
  • +1 if Site A is not a transporter pad.
  • +1 if Site B is not a transporter pad.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.
interact.chatSummary: Interact with an object. Complex tasks need a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.
prepare.chatSummary: Ready yourself for a rolled task.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.
restore.chatSummary: Resolve minor issues with ship systems.

impulse.name: Impulse
impulse.description:
Move 2 zones.

If you only move 1 zone, you may reduce the Momentum cost of movement through difficult or hazardous terrain by 1.
impulse.chatSummary: Move 2 zones. Moving 1 zone reduces terrain Momentum cost by 1.

thrusters.name: Thrusters
thrusters.description: Move around within your current zone. You may make Contact with another ship safely, such as docking or landing.
thrusters.chatSummary: Move within your zone. Dock or land safely.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description:
Roll vs Difficulty 2 to:
  • create a trait
  • change a trait
  • or remove a trait
Examples include: careful positioning, skilled maneuvering.
createTrait.chatSummary: Difficulty 2. Create, change, or remove a trait.

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.
override.chatSummary: Take a non-Command station action at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.
ready.chatSummary: Defer a major action until a chosen trigger occurs.

attackPattern.name: Attack Pattern
attackPattern.description: Assist each attack until your next turn with Control + Conn. All incoming attacks have -1 Difficulty until your next turn.
attackPattern.chatSummary: Assist all attacks until next turn. Incoming attack difficulty -1.

evasiveAction.name: Evasive Action
evasiveAction.description:
Until your next turn, all incoming attacks are opposed rolls vs your Daring + Conn assisted by the ship's Structure + Conn.If you win the opposed roll, you may move the ship 1 zone.

All attacks made by your ship suffer +1 Difficulty until your next turn.

You may not take this action at the same time as Defensive Fire.
evasiveAction.chatSummary: Opposed roll vs incoming attacks. Your attacks +1 Difficulty until next turn.

maneuver.name: Maneuver
maneuver.description: Roll Control + Conn vs Difficulty 0 to generate momentum, especially when crossing difficult terrain.

ram.name: Ram
ram.description: Move into Contact with a target at Close range. Perform an Attack with Daring + Conn assisted by the ship's Engines + Conn vs Difficulty 2. Each ship suffers damage equal to the other's scale + half the number of zones moved before collision. This attack is Devastating, Piercing, and Intense.
ram.chatSummary: Daring + Conn, Difficulty 2. Both ships take scale + half-zones damage.

warp.name: Warp
warp.description: Requires Reserve Power. You must take a Prepare minor action first. Roll Control + Conn assisted by the ship's Engines + Conn vs Difficulty 1. On success, move a number of zones equal to Engines or leave the battlefield.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.
interact.chatSummary: Interact with an object. Complex tasks need a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.
prepare.chatSummary: Ready yourself for a rolled task.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.
restore.chatSummary: Resolve minor issues with ship systems.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 to create a trait, change a trait, or remove a trait (e.g. plotting a careful course, studying the terrain).
createTrait.chatSummary: Difficulty 2. Create, change, or remove a trait.

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.
override.chatSummary: Take a non-Command station action at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.
ready.chatSummary: Defer a major action until a chosen trigger occurs.

changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.
interact.chatSummary: Interact with an object. Complex tasks need a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.
prepare.chatSummary: Ready yourself for a rolled task.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.
restore.chatSummary: Resolve minor issues with ship systems.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 to create a trait, change a trait, or remove a trait (e.g. modifications to ship systems).
createTrait.chatSummary: Difficulty 2. Create, change, or remove a trait.

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.
override.chatSummary: Take a non-Command station action at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.
ready.chatSummary: Defer a major action until a chosen trigger occurs.

damageControl.name: Damage Control
damageControl.description: Repair a damaged or disabled system. Roll Presence + Engineering assisted by the ship's Structure + Engineering vs Difficulty 2. A damaged system is repaired. A disabled system becomes damaged instead.
damageControl.chatSummary: Presence + Engineering, Difficulty 2. Repair a damaged/disabled system.

regainPower.name: Regain Power
regainPower.description: Roll Control + Engineering vs Difficulty 1. On success, restore Reserve Power. May succeed at a cost, where complications reprsent subsystems shutting down.

regenerateShields.name: Regenerate Shields
regenerateShields.description: Requires Reserve Power. Roll Control + Engineering vs Difficulty 2. On success, regain shields equal to the ship's Security score.
regenerateShields.chatSummary: Requires Reserve Power. Difficulty 2. Regain shields equal to Security.

reroutePower.name: Reroute Power
reroutePower.description: No roll required. Move Reserve Power from Engines to any other system, or vice versa. You may not move power from other systems.
reroutePower.chatSummary: Move Reserve Power between Engines and another system.

transport.name: Transport
transport.description:
Beam people or objects over distances. Roll Control + Engineering assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science. Base Difficulty 0.
  • +1 if away from transporter room.
  • +1 if Site A is not a transporter pad.
  • +1 if Site B is not a transporter pad.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.
interact.chatSummary: Interact with an object. Complex tasks need a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.
prepare.chatSummary: Ready yourself for a rolled task.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.
restore.chatSummary: Resolve minor issues with ship systems.

calibrateSensors.name: Calibrate Sensors
calibrateSensors.description: On your next Sensor Operations action, you may either ignore a single trait affecting the task, or re-roll 1d20.
calibrateSensors.chatSummary: Ignore a single trait affecting a Sensors task, or re-roll a d20.

launchProbe.name: Launch Probe
launchProbe.description:
Select a single zone anywhere within Long range and launch a sensor probe to that zone. Sensor Operations major actions may determine range from the probe's location rather than the ship.

The probe can be targeted as if a Small Craft and is destroyed if it takes any damage.
launchProbe.chatSummary: Launch probe to Long range. Sensor Operations actions use probe's location.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description:
Roll vs Difficulty 2 to:
  • create a trait
  • change a trait
  • or remove a trait
For example, extra information gathered from sensor scans.
createTrait.chatSummary: Difficulty 2. Create, change, or remove a trait.

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.
override.chatSummary: Take a non-Command station action at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.
ready.chatSummary: Defer a major action until a chosen trigger occurs.

reveal.name: Reveal
reveal.description:
You scan for the trace signals that may reveal the tell-tale presence of a cloaked vessel, or a vessel concealed by some other phenomenon. Attempt a Reason + Science task, assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science, with a Difficulty of 3.

If you succeed, and there is a hidden vessel within Long range, you reveal which zone it is in (if there are multiple, this only reveals one such vessel, chosen at random). Until that vessel moves, your ship may attack that hidden vessel, but attacks increase the Difficulty by 2.
reveal.chatSummary: Reason + Science (ship Sensors + Science), Difficulty 3. Reveal hidden vessel's zone; attacks vs it are possible until it moves, but at +2 Difficulty.

scanForWeakness.name: Scan for Weakness
scanForWeakness.description:
You scan an enemy vessel, looking for vulnerabilities. Choose a single vessel and attempt a Difficulty 2 Control + Science task, assisted by the ship's Sensors + Security.

On success, the next attack against that ship either increases its damage by 2 or gains the Piercing quality (ignoring the target's resistance).
scanForWeakness.chatSummary: Control + Science (ship Sensors + Security), Difficulty 2. The next attack vs that ship is +2 damage or gains Piercing.

sensorSweep.name: Sensor Sweep
sensorSweep.description: Scan for nearby objects, energy signatures, and anomalies. Attempt a Difficulty 1 Reason + Science task, assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science. On success, learn basic information about a single zone. Spend Momentum for more detail.
sensorSweep.chatSummary: Reason + Science (ship Sensors + Science), Difficulty 1. Learn about nearby objects and anomalies.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.
interact.chatSummary: Interact with an object. Complex tasks need a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description:
Spend time setting up a task. At tactical, this action can also be used to raise or lower the ship's shields, or to arm or disarm the ship's weapons.
  • Shields: When the shields are lowered, the ship's maximum shields are 0. When raised, they are restored to their normal maximum, or to their previous total if the ship has taken any damage this scene.
  • Weapons: The ship may only make Weapons Attacks if the weapons are armed. However, enemy ships can detect whether your ship's weapons are armed.
prepare.chatSummary: Ready a task, raise/lower shields, or arm/disarm weapons.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.
restore.chatSummary: Resolve minor issues with ship systems.

calibrateWeapons.name: Calibrate Weapons
calibrateWeapons.description: Recalibrate the ship's weapons. On your next attack with the ship’s weapons, increase the weapon’s damage by 1.
calibrateWeapons.chatSummary: Next ship weapon attack increases damage by 1.

targetingSolution.name: Targeting Solution
targetingSolution.description: You lock targeting sensors onto an enemy vessel. Select a single enemy vessel within Long range. On the next attack against that vessel, either re-roll a d20 on the task, or choose which of the target's systems are hit by the attack, rather than rolling.
targetingSolution.chatSummary: Select a target within Long range. Next attack: re-roll 1d20 or choose hit systems.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description:
Roll vs Difficulty 2 to:
  • create a trait
  • change a trait
  • remove a trait
Examples include weapon modifications and targeting solutions.
createTrait.chatSummary: Difficulty 2. Create, change, or remove a trait.

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.
override.chatSummary: Take a non-Command station action at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.
ready.chatSummary: Defer a major action until a chosen trigger occurs.

defensiveFire.name: Defensive Fire
defensiveFire.description:
Choose a single energy weapon on your ship. Until your next turn, any enemy attack against your ship becomes an opposed task, opposed by your Daring + Security, assisted by the ship's Weapons + Security.

If you succeed, you may spend 2 Momentum to counterattack, inflicting your weapon's damage against the attacker.

You cannot take this action if the ship is currently benefitting from Evasive Action.
defensiveFire.chatSummary: Oppose enemy attacks. On success, spend 2 Momentum to counterattack.

fire.name: Fire
fire.description: Attack an enemy ship or target with the ship's weapons. Roll Control + Security assisted by the ship's Weapons + Security. Base Difficulty 2 for energy weapons. Difficulty 3 for torpedoes.

modulateShields.name: Modulate Shields
modulateShields.description:
No roll required. Spend 1 Reserve Power. Until the start of your next turn, the ship gains +2 Resistance against energy weapons.

You cannot use this acion if shields are at 0.
modulateShields.chatSummary: Spend 1 Reserve Power. +2 Resistance vs energy weapons until the next turn.

tractorBeam.name: Tractor Beam
tractorBeam.description: Engage a tractor beam on a nearby object or vessel within Close range. Attempt a Control + Security task at Difficulty 2, assisted by the ship's Structure + Security. On success, the target cannot move. Attempts to escape use a Difficulty equal to your ship's tractor beam strength.
tractorBeam.chatSummary: Control + Security (ship Structure + Security), Difficulty 2, Close range. On success, target is immobilized. Escape Difficulty equals your tractor beam strength.

aim.name: Aim
aim.description: When you make an Attack this turn, you may re-roll a single d20 on the task roll.
aim.chatSummary: Re-roll 1d20 on your next Attack this turn.

drawItem.name: Draw Item
drawItem.description: Pick up an item within Reach or draw an item you are carrying. If using the item doesn’t require a task to use, you can use it immediately as part of this minor action.
drawItem.chatSummary: Pick up or draw an item. Use immediately if no task required.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Interact with an object in the environment. Complex actions require a major action.
interact.chatSummary: Interact with an object in the environment.

movement.name: Move
movement.description: Move up to one zone, to any point within Medium range. You cannot take this minor action in the same turn as the Sprint major action. If there are any enemies within Reach of you, you cannot perform this action.
movement.chatSummary: Move up to 1 zone (Medium range). Not with Sprint or enemies in Reach.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Some items require this minor action before they can be used, and some major actions require this minor action before they can be attempted. Sometimes items will grant special benefits if this action is used before performing a task.
prepare.chatSummary: Prepare for a task or ready an item for use.

standDropProne.name: Stand/Drop Prone
standDropProne.description: Drop to the ground or stand up. While prone, Ranged attacks against you have +1 Difficulty, and if you are in Cover, you gain an additional +1 Protection. However, Melee Attacks and Ranged Attacks at Close range gain 2 bonus Momentum against you, and you cannot attempt any movement-related major actions. Bonus Momentum cannot be saved. You cannot Stand and Drop Prone in the same turn.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

attack.name: Attack
attack.description:
Attack an enemy or other viable target and attempt to injure them. Ranged attacks use Control + Security at Difficulty 2. Melee attacks use Daring + Security at Difficulty 1.

Attack tasks are opposed if the Enemy is able to defend themselves in melee or has cover at range. On a success, the target must take stress equal to the weapon's severity or suffer an Injury and become Defeated.
attack.chatSummary: Attack an enemy to injure them or deal stress equal to the weapon's severity.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description:
Roll vs Difficulty 2 (usually) to:
  • Create a trait
  • Change a trait
  • Remove a trait
Examples include: cover, difficult terrain, distractions, or other environmental effects.
createTrait.chatSummary: Difficulty 2. Create, change, or remove a trait.

direct.name: Direct
direct.description: May only be attempted by one character on each side in a position of authority. Spend 1 Momentum and select one ally who can hear you. They may immediately attempt a single major action with no +1 Difficulty. Assist them with your Control + Command.
direct.chatSummary: Spend 1 Momentum. An ally takes a major action. You assist with Control + Command.

firstAid.name: First Aid
firstAid.description:
Attempt to revive a Defeated character within reach. Perform a Daring + Medicine task, Difficulty 2. If successful, the character is no longer Defeated.

Alternatively, treat the Injury with a Difficulty equal to its severity. If successful, the injury has been treated and imposes no penalty for the rest of the scene, but is not removed until long term care is given.
firstAid.chatSummary: Daring + Medicine, Difficulty 2. Revive a Defeated character or treat their Injury.

guard.name: Guard
guard.description:
Defend yourself, preparing for an attack. This is an Insight + Security task, Difficulty 0.

Success increases the Difficulty of any attacks against you by 1 until the start of your next turn.

You may guard an ally within reach instead of yourself at +1 Difficulty.
guard.chatSummary: Insight + Security, Difficulty 0. +1 Difficulty to attacks against you.

otherTasks.name: Other Tasks
otherTasks.description: Do something not described here. GM will tell you how to do it.
otherTasks.chatSummary: Perform a task not otherwise listed.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform it when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.
ready.chatSummary: Defer a major action until a chosen trigger occurs.

sprint.name: Sprint
sprint.description: Move two zones, to any point within Long range. If there is difficult or hazardous terrain, you may attempt a Fitness + Conn task with Difficulty 0 as part of this action, to generate Momentum to cross the terrain.
sprint.chatSummary: Move 2 zones (Long range). Fitness + Conn Difficulty 0 for terrain.

personal.accurate: If you perform the Aim minor action before making an attack with this weapon, you may re-roll up to two d20s in your dice pool, rather than only one.
personal.area: When you succeed at an attack, additional targets in the same zone may be hit by spending 1 Momentum for each additional target (Repeatable). This attack may Succeed at Cost.
personal.charge: If you perform the Prepare minor action before attacking with this weapon, you may add one of the following qualities to the attack: Area, Intense, or Piercing. If you choose Area, the attack's severity is reduced by 1.
personal.cumbersome: You cannot attack with a cumbersome weapon unless you take the Prepare minor action on the same turn.
personal.deadly: This weapon can kill a target.
personal.debilitating: The Difficulty to treat or to heal injuries caused by this weapon is increased by 1.
personal.stun: The weapon can incapacitate a target.
personal.grenade: You can attack a target at up to Medium range and have enough grenades for three separate attacks.
personal.hiddenx: You can use a minor action to conceal this weapon. Any search for the weapon requires an Insight or Reason + Security task with a Difficulty of X.
personal.inaccurate: The weapon is imprecise and clumsy. You do not benefit from the Aim minor action when making an attack with this weapon.
personal.intense: When making an attack with this weapon, you may increase the severity by spending 1 Momentum, rather than 2 (Repeatable).
personal.piercing: A successful attack with this weapon ignores the target's Protection rating.
personal.piercingx: A successful attack with this weapon ignores the target's Protection rating.

starship.area: This weapon impacts a wider area and can affect several targets at once. When you succeed at an attack, additional targets in the same zone may be hit by spending 1 Momentum for each additional target (Repeatable). This attack may Succeed at Cost (see page 259).
starship.calibration: This weapon needs to be calibrated before firing. The weapon cannot be fired unless a Prepare minor action is performed before the Attack major action in the same turn.
starship.cumbersome: This weapon is difficult to bring to bear against a target, increasing the Difficulty of an attack made using it by 1. If this weapon is a torpedo, it cannot be fired as part of a salvo (see page 229 for more information on salvos).
starship.dampening: This attack drains the target's reserve power, causing widespread disruption. If the target has Reserve Power available, it loses Reserve Power.
starship.depleting: This attack strips away the protective layers of deflector shields. The attack cannot cause the ship to be shaken, but the Momentum cost to increase damage is reduced to 1.
starship.devastating: Tasks to repair breaches caused by this weapon increase in Difficulty by 1.
starship.hiddenx: This weapon system is concealed from scans. When the weapon is hidden, it cannot be detected unless a specific scan is attempted; this scan is a task with a Difficulty of X.
starship.highyield: This weapon inflicts massive damage to energy vessels. If the attack inflicts one or more breaches to a system, it inflicts one additional breach (or steps up the Potency of a breach inflicted by 1—attacker's choice).
starship.intense: When making an attack with this weapon, you may increase the damage by 1 by spending 1 Momentum, rather than 2. This is Repeatable.
starship.jamming: This weapon sends out a scattering field, disrupting the target's sensors or communications systems. Until the end of the current round, the target increases the Difficulty for all tasks assisted by Communications and Sensors by 1.
starship.persistent: When this weapon hits, you may spend 1–3 Momentum. At the end of each round, the target suffers half the weapon's damage rating (round up) again. This effect lasts for a number of rounds equal to the Momentum spent.
starship.piercing: The weapon is effective at cutting through layers of protection. When this weapon makes an attack, any Resistance the target has is ignored.
starship.slowing: Until the end of the current round, characters aboard the target vessel cannot use the Keep the Initiative Momentum spend.
starship.spread: Attacks with this weapon reduce the cost of the Devastating Attack Momentum spend to 1. This is Repeatable.
starship.versatile: If an attack with this weapon is successful, it gains bonus Momentum points. Bonus Momentum cannot be saved.
starship.versatilex: If an attack with this weapon is successful, it gains X points of bonus Momentum. Bonus Momentum cannot be saved.

shaken.title: SHAKEN!
shaken.flavour: The ship's shields have dropped to half! Choose a minor damage effect:
shaken.rollForItHint: Roll a d20 on the Minor Damage table to determine the effect.
shaken.breachDescription: If the ship was shaken this turn, the ship gains a breach.

brace.label: Brace for Impact!
brace.description: On the next turn this ship (or a character aboard this ship) takes, they may not take a major action.

losingPower.label: Losing Power!
losingPower.description: If you have Reserve Power, lose the use of Reserve Power. The next time you attempt to Regain Power, the Difficulty is increased by 1.

difficulty.label: Difficulty
difficulty.desc: The extended task's Difficulty is increased or reduced by 1.

hindrance.label: Hindrance
hindrance.desc: The extended task's Resistance is increased or reduced by 1, to a minimum of 0.

progress.label: Progress
progress.desc: The next roll made against the problem gains +2 Impact.

sceneEffect.label: Effect on the Scene
sceneEffect.desc: The problem reduces the Difficulty of tasks elsewhere in the scene, or increases the Difficulty of an enemy's tasks.

createTrait.label: Create a Trait
createTrait.desc: A trait is created, removed, or changed. A trait created or altered by an extended task is more persistent and more difficult to affect than one created by an ordinary task or by Momentum.

event.label: Event
event.desc: The problem is tied to specific occurrences in the scene, with each breakthrough altering the state of the scene. Perhaps enemies grow more numerous, desperate, or dangerous with each breakthrough until the problem is overcome.

uncertainty.label: Uncertainty
uncertainty.desc: Some tasks associated with the consequence increase their complication range by 1 (i.e., from 20, to 19–20).

difficulty.label: Difficulty
difficulty.desc: Some tasks associated with the consequence become more difficult, adding 1 to their Difficulty.

instability.label: Instability
instability.desc: The consequence grows more precarious. This may reduce the consequence's Resistance (if any) by 2 or add 2 to the Trouble rating.

escalation.label: Escalation
escalation.desc: The setback adds 2 Threat, to be used immediately or saved for some later problem.

createTrait.label: Create Trait
createTrait.desc: The setback creates a trait which hinders the player characters or helps the enemy.

event.label: Event
event.desc: The setback triggers some activity or change in the scene, such as the arrival or departure of specific individuals, a change in enemy tactics, a new danger, or even inflicting Stress on a player character.

clearAllDialog.title: Clear All Trackers
clearAllDialog.message: Delete all extended task trackers? This cannot be undone.

warnings.noActiveGM: No active GM is available to process this update.
